description: Scratchpad v1 sweep: a new toolset = medium + an engine frame-stack (assume/commit/retract) to auto-manage case-split state, run on puzzles 001/002/004/003, n=1.
motivation: Test whether delegating branch-bookkeeping to the engine (removing manual snapshot/restore) extends the ceiling or cuts cost.
formal_results: 001 solved $1.50/25; 002 solved $2.58/37 (28% cheaper than medium, 0 frame primitives used); 004 unsolved $21.17/165 (11 assume/retract pairs, 0 commits - 29% cheaper than medium at the same failure); 003 (25x30) no data (rate-limited).
results_description: Auto-bookkeeping is real and cheaper at the failure boundary, but the model never commits a frame - it tests hypotheses that all fail and makes no forward progress.
conclusions: Frame auto-delegation saves money but does not break the 15x15 ceiling; frames are used but ineffective. Motivates v2 with engine-level lookahead (try_both).
